sourceName: WHO Director-General media briefing on hantavirus, 12 May 2026
notes: Manual tracker dataset for the MV Hondius Andes virus outbreak. Latest headline count: 11 total cases, including 9 confirmed Andes virus infections, 2 probable cases, 0 suspected cases, and 3 deaths. WHO recommends monitoring through 21 June 2026, 42 days from last exposure on 10 May 2026.

ship.name: MV Hondius
ship.lastKnownStatus: Passengers have disembarked and left Tenerife; MV Hondius is on its way to the Netherlands.

route.event: Reported voyage departure point for Antarctic and Atlantic itinerary.
Earliest illness onset date reported in WHO outbreak notice.
Cluster of severe respiratory illness reported to WHO.
Medical evacuation of suspected and confirmed patients.

route.location: Port of Granadilla, Tenerife, Canary Islands
route.event: MV Hondius arrived for disembarkation and repatriation operations.
WHO stated the ship was on its way to the Netherlands after passengers left Tenerife.

passengerStatus.note: Repatriated and crew-onboard counts are tracker operational counts based on public reporting and WHO/ECDC statements. Case totals use the WHO 12 May 2026 briefing.

timeline.title: Earliest reported illness onset
timeline.detail: WHO later reported illness onset among cases occurred between 6 and 28 April 2026.

timeline.title: WHO notified of cluster
timeline.detail: A cluster of severe respiratory illness aboard MV Hondius was reported to WHO.

timeline.title: WHO publishes first Disease Outbreak News
timeline.detail: WHO reported seven cases, including two laboratory-confirmed hantavirus cases and three deaths.

timeline.title: CDC issues MV Hondius statement
timeline.detail: CDC said the risk to the American public was extremely low and linked readers to hantavirus and Andes virus information.

timeline.title: WHO update identifies Andes virus
timeline.detail: WHO reported eight total cases, including six confirmed and two probable cases, with all confirmed cases identified as Andes virus.

timeline.title: MV Hondius arrives in Tenerife
timeline.detail: The ship arrived at the port of Granadilla, Tenerife, for disembarkation and repatriation.

timeline.title: WHO reports 11 cases and 3 deaths
timeline.detail: WHO reported 11 cases among passengers or crew: 9 confirmed Andes virus infections, 2 probable cases, and 3 deaths.

timeline.title: Tracker dataset verified
timeline.detail: Hantavirus Tracker manually verified the 11-case headline count and 42-day monitoring window.
